content.text: Services Week is back 29 June to 3 July 2026 and the theme this year is personalised public services. Effective public services emerge from innovative thinking, a clear understanding of what people need, and working closely across teams and specialisms. This week provides the community opportunity to explore the connections between services providers and the communities they support to improve public services.

Are you a digital and data professional in the public sector? Services Week brings people together to share, learn and collaborate, exploring how technology powered services and stronger community connections can improve public services.
If you want to add a session to this year’s Services Week complete the session proposal survey by the deadline of 19 June. You can also see how the programme is shaping up below.

Now in its eighth year, Services Week is a yearly event hosted by the Government Digital and Data community, bringing people from different areas across the public sector together to share best practice and learn from one another, all with the aim of improving public services. You can read more about Services Week here.

Last year there were over 90 sessions from 35 different organisations and approximately 7500 people dialled into events over the week. Services Week has significant reach across the public sector. It is an important part of the calendar for lots of service teams across government. This year we want to make it bigger and better than ever, and we need your help to do that.

This year’s Services Week focuses on personalising public services. The theme reflects our ambition to enable more joined up services, ones that cross departmental or organisational boundaries but feel seamless to the user.

Supporting this kind of joined up delivery is at the heart of Services Week, and we are really looking forward to seeing the sessions you have in store. We welcome those that embrace this theme, as well as sessions focused more broadly on designing high-quality experiences and showcasing the excellent work your teams are delivering.
Services Week is a fantastic opportunity to:

If you are interested in hosting a session at this year’s event, complete the session proposal survey by 19 June. The form allows you to outline your proposed session ready for publication in the live agenda document here. Be sure to submit your proposal early to maximise sign-ups for your session.

This year we have set up a shared folder for session hosts, where you can find instructions on how to set your session up, a slide deck template for Services Week 2026, our code of conduct, helpful tips to make your session more accessible and so much more.
You can also join our cross-government Slack channel for all the latest information and resources.
If you have any questions, contact the Services Week team.
